desc:
The forest is thinning a little here, and it is finally possible to struggle
into the thick undergrowth to the north. A lone tree here seems to rustle and
creek more than those deep in the heart of the forest, with many of its leaves
fluttering to the ground below.
name: At the Edge of the Forest

desc:
Most of the trees in this forest are extremely large, however, the one that
stands here does not seem to be like the others. It has not grown very tall, and
its trunk seems to have grown around itself, making it look extremely twisted.
At the base of the tree, the tangled roots and rotten leaves seem to obscure a
small hole.
name: A Twisted Tree

desc:
Of the many tall trees that fill these woods, one stands particularly high. It
has a huge base with the size of a large cabin, the branches themselves are the
size of small trees. Further within the forest, towards the west, trees
identical to this one loom in abundance. The sky is masked from sight with the
large branches and leaves.
name: Beneath a Large Tree

desc:
The trees around here have grown closer to the ground than they do in other
areas within the forest. Their branches almost touch the ground, and in some
spots there are marks from where they have scraped the ground, pushed by the
wind. The grass has grown a little higher here, from the water that is trapped
on the forest floor during rainy seasons.
name: Forest Floor

desc:
The ground here is cooled by both the air that caresses it, and the lush green
grass that catches each morning's dew. There is little to see here, other than a
variety of trees, with every branch, twig, and leaf looking exactly like all the
others.
name: Forest Hills

desc:
A small hill slopes up here, covered with grass and the roots of trees that grow
at an angle along the hill. There is a small hole at the base of the incline,
almost covered by two tree trunks growing near each other. A few wild flowers
grow across the side of the hill, their purple petals and long green stems
blending beautifully with the colour of the leaves on the trees.
name: Base of the Forest Hill

desc:
The few scattered trees of the pathway have now closed in to form a giant
forest. The air seems almost still, but for a gentle breeze, which rustles
amongst the branches. Where once the path was grassy, now you stand upon ground
littered with dead twigs, and decaying leaves, and interrupted in places by
large, twisted roots, which protrude from the earth.
name: Entrance to a Large Forest

desc:
A haunting chime can be heard as a breeze slowly picks up through the branches
of the trees. The sign at the entrance of this forest may have some truth in it
as a definite sound seems to emanate from nowhere. Branches from many different
trees also have grown closer and closer towards the ground, almost touching it.
name: Enchanted Forest

desc:
One tree stands out among the rest in this area of the forest, it has large
branches with many different kinds of coloured leaves. The ground below it is
covered with the large seeds of its making. Foliage from the tree covers the
area completely, obscuring any sight of the sky, and the roots of the tree are
almost as thick as its branches, spreading out to some distance, to soak up
every drop of available water. No bush or sapling can compete with this giant,
which stands alone, and majestic.
name: At a Large Lovely Tree

desc:
Woods that once were so close, it was difficult to distinguish which branch grew
from which tree, now have opened up to reveal the sky. The forest has formed a
circle where no foliage dare enter. Grass closely hugs the border of the circle,
making the boundary even more defined. All living beings within this forest
avoid this area, as if it were somehow held sacred.
name: Opening in the Forest

desc:
A large tree stands almost as a silhoutte here, with large green and brown
leaves covering it entirely. The woodland behind grows even denser, the deeper
one penetrates the forest, and a number of large branches lie on the ground
here, perhaps brought down by some heavy wind.
name: At an Old Tree

desc:
The woods continue to grow close together and crowd in on each other. This, and
the dense foliage, make it difficult to tell what is up within the trees. A few
notches have been carved here in the bark of the tree, like an ancient hand hold
in a mountainside.
name: Large Tree

desc:
The woodland is filled with trees, that are extremely close to one another. This
forest has the look of being long left untouched, except for some marks on the
trees. A sound is present that resembles a moan caused by wind rustling through
branches.
name: Some Woods

desc:
There seems to be no light entering this part of the woods. Trees have covered
all the land here with their large branches and their many leaves. The grass on
the ground is moist with the water that is trapped from evaporating with a
morning sun.
name: Dimly Lit Woods

desc:
A couple of trees stand here close together, with their branches so interwined
that they look like they are hugging. Another tree stands alone however, with
its dead leaves and dead trunk. The other parts of the woodland keep a fair
distance from this dead and strangely large tree.
name: Clump of Trees

desc:
Parts of the interior of this tree are dead and decaying, giving off a rank
odour. However, there is sufficient living wood remaining to maintain a strong
structure, despite the rotting leaf litter and dead limbs, which lie all over
the floor.
name: Inside a Decaying Tree

desc:
The branches high up in the trees are rustling around much more than they did
before, with the growing wind slapping them. Sounds can be heard coming from way
up within the trees, however it is difficult to distinguish what the noises are.
Many leaves lie on the ground, yet, there are many more up in the trees that
make it impossible to see what is up there.
name: Rustling Woods

desc:
Many noises can be heard coming from above, within the large foliage of the
forest. Dangling from a branch, there appear to be some wooden pipes tied
together, with holes in them. The small breeze that moves through them creates
the most eerie noises. It is nothing more than a child's wind chime toy.
name: Loud Forest

desc:
Many different items sit in this room on shelves supported by branches of the
tree. There are a few larger items that rest on the floor beside a large counter
that stands in the middle of the room. There is a board on the counter that
lists many prices of different things, however, they have been erased and
rewritten so many times, it is difficult to tell what may be written on them.
name: General Store

desc:
The bridge is well tied, with many thick and well made ropes wrapped around the
different trees of the village. The rather narrow bridge makes passage tricky,
though it does not wobble in the wind. One board, in the middle, creaks softly
when pressure is upon it.
name: Rope Bridge

desc:
There are a lot of wooden planks transformed into a platform in these huge
crowded trees. The ground is far below, and small forest animals go about the
trees unaware of the village above. A rope bridge connects the trees to small
homes made of wood. The branches and leaves of the tree conceal it from below.
The wooden rail along the platform disappears as three rope bridges begin to the
north, south and west.
name: Entrance to a Tree Village

desc:
Aloft high in the tree, constructed of a few small boards, stands a small perch.
Like a bird's nest, the entire village can be seen from this height, including
some of the houses that from below cannot be found. Branches criss and cross
below here, making a natural ladder, with a square made out of rope as the only
guard rail for an experienced tree climber. The entire forest, and most of the
village can be seen and watched from up within this high tree.
name: Archer's Post

desc:
Animal furs hang along the walls of the home, their fur now only a decoration. A
large bear skin lies on the floor near the entrance, its mouth grimly shut and
facing the door. A window along the north side exposes the ground below, however
the branches and leaves provide excellent cover.
name: Large Lovely Home

desc:
The bridge has many boards tied together tightly, creating a very sturdy, and
well made bridge. The wood is painted a greenish and brownish colour, most
likely to make it nearly invisibile to the ground below. There are many ropes
tied to different branches to keep it very sturdy. The rail is a thick rope
wrapped around the trunk of two trees.
name: Rope Bridge

desc:
A large bed made of furs lies in one of the corners of the room, with a large
goose down pillow and many smaller ones piled up on top of it. A large bow and
quiver rests neatly in the opposite corner of the house, with arrows that have
beautifully coloured feathers within them. Branches run up along the back wall,
providing more evidence that the house is supported solely by the trees.
name: Home within the Trees

desc:
A large oven is embedded in the side of possibly the largest tree in this
forest. Its bricks have decayed from a bright red colour, to one of blackened
grey. There are pastries, wrapped delicately, sitting on shelves. A small chair
and table sit beside the door of the house. The smells coming from the house
have a taste in the mouth like that of fresh bread, pies, and cakes.
name: Baker's Home

desc:
There are wooden floors of different sizes, heights, and shapes all over this
tree. A small building is supported among the massive branches to the north,
whilst to the south and east, rope bridges reach out toward the nearby trees.
Along the platform and the bridges are rails of soft wood, supported by
interlocking branches, and tightly bound ropes.
name: Large Tree

desc:
An intersection stands within the trees, made with tightly bound ropes and
sturdy wood for the bridge. Buildings clutter all the trees nearby, with many
rope bridges connecting them. The ground seems a far cry down below, with the
branches of trees around it concealing it almost totally from sight. A large
tree looms towards the west, its massive dead branches appear to be barely
hanging onto it.
name: Rope Bridge

desc:
Unlike most of the others in this part of the forest, this tree is quite
unhealthy. Much of the wood is dead and some decaying, yet enough of the living
heartwood remains, to maintain a strong platform, among the sagging branches.
Nevertheless, much of the coarse bark on the trunk is peeling as if diseased.
name: At a Large Decaying Tree

desc:
The platform is wide enough for many people to stand upon, with wooden rails
enclosing it. This wooden structure is attached to an extremely large tree, and
nearby, in another large oak, a building nestles among the branches. The leaves
all around rustle in the soft wind, and the ground below cannot be seen through
the thick foliage. Indeed, this place must be well hidden from the forest floor.
name: Tree Platform

desc:
Furs of many different animals hang from hooks in the wall, while, along the
opposite wall, animal skins and leather lie on shelves. A large counter, with a
wooden board hanging on the front, sits in the middle of the room. The board
lists many prices of different items for sale, however, prices have been crossed
out and new ones put in their place so many times, that it is difficult to read
them.
name: Village Clothery

desc:
The rope bridge makes a turn in mid air, creating an artwork of tied rope to
keep it sturdy. It links a couple of different trees together, and with the
extremely intricate rope pattern, allows for the bridge to change its course
without having to find yet another tree. Even in fairly strong winds, the bridge
hardly sways at all.
name: Rope Bridge

desc:
The bridge has many boards tied together tightly, creating a very sturdy, and
well made bridge. The wood is painted a greenish and brownish colour, most
likely to make it nearly invisibile to the ground below. There are many ropes
tied to different branches to keep it very sturdy. The rail is a thick rope
wrapped around the trunk of two trees.
name: Rope Bridge

desc:
A few chairs stand in the middle of the room, facing some paintings that hang
along the wall depicting the building of the village. A wolf skin covers the
floor, greeting people as they walk in. Hanging in a corner is a lit lantern,
with a small bucket of water sitting beneath it. The light from the lantern
flickers on the wall, making the most beautiful dances.
name: House in the Trees

desc:
The hole does not go back very far, but the rear seems to be a small fox den.
Tiny bones clutter the corners, with a few pieces of fur left almong them, and
tiny claw marks can be seen all over the ground, among the stout roots, which
break through the soil and stretch up to the roof of the chamber. It would seem
that the tree roots are the only thing which maintains the roof of this tiny
earthy hollow.
name: Earthy Hollow

desc:
The ground above has rotted away from beside the tree to reveal this small hole.
The carcass of a rotting animal along with the smell of rotten tree limbs and
leaves, makes the overall smell of the hole very pungent. A few wild flowers
grow along the sides near the top of the hole, hiding the outside from view.
name: Under the Twisted Tree

desc:
Like the edge of a treacherous mountainside, the ground is filled with marks and
slashes. Tiny little streams course through the little valleys formed by the
slashes on the floor. From this small cavern there are but two ways to journey.
To the north lies an underground stream, whilst another passage climbs almost
vertically from here.
name: Damp Tunnel
